name: Supply-Chain Provenance
description: Verifiable supply-chain provenance: an ITEM is a factory-born sovereign cell whose installed CellProgram enforces single-custodianship as a CONSERVATION law. A custody HANDOFF is a cap-attenuated transfer that strictly advances a provenance epoch (no replay) and appends a tamper-evident receipt link; the provenance is the on-cell hash chain (link_i = blake3(prev || event)), which a third party PROVES by re-derivation. A FORGED handoff is REFUSED by the verified executor on two teeth: the cap-graph (no custody cap reaching the item) and the actor-bound register (AnyOf[Immutable, SenderInSlot]). Composes existing slot-caveat atoms only; no new Lean module.

service.interface: ProvenanceService (src/service.rs) — a typed InterfaceDescriptor driven through the invoke() front door

methods.mint: Replayable, Signature — inaugurate the sole custodian (CUSTODIAN=signer, EPOCH 0->1, genesis link)
methods.handoff: Replayable, Signature — the incoming holder accepts custody (advance the actor-bound baton to the signer, strict-mono EPOCH, WriteOnce link, append-only HEAD)
methods.view: Serviced, None — the named OFE seam: read the committed custody state + provenance chain, never desugared

card.source: card::provenance_card_value (src/card.rs) — a renderer-independent deos.ui.* view-tree (pure serde_json)
card.shape: vstack[ header, bind(EPOCH_SLOT), button(mint), button(handoff), button(view) ]

derived.summary: derived::summarize (src/derived.rs) — a provenance summary is a derived view f(events): current custodian, handoff count, epoch, chain tip, conserved
derived.derived_cell: derived::bind_shipment_manifest / verify_shipment_manifest — a shipment-manifest derived cell over a roster of item source cells, grounded on dregg_cell::derived (DerivationSpec + Aggregate::Count); a forged (over-counted) or stale (un-re-derived) manifest fails verify_derivation (the Lean rung Dregg2/Deos/DerivedCell.lean)
derived.attested_query: derived::attested_custody_log / verify_attested_custody_log — a non-omission completeness certificate over the custody-handoff log, grounded on dregg_query (the receipt-log MMR, the Rust embodiment of Dregg2/Lightclient/MMR.lean's server_cannot_omit_position); a tampered or omitted handoff breaks the certificate

deos.mount: register_deos (src/lib.rs) — folds the composed DeosApp into the StarbridgeAppContext affordance registry; DeosApp::mount yields the axum router
deos.publish: Signature (the VERIFIER tier — the item cell is a dregg:// sturdyref a regulator on another federation reacquires to re-derive the custody chain)

affordances.view_provenance: cap-only, Signature (a VERIFIER reads + re-derives the chain)
affordances.accept_custody: GATED (cap and state), Either — minted precondition (EPOCH >= 1); the fire submits the FULL handoff (CUSTODIAN=signer + strict-mono EPOCH + WriteOnce link + append-only HEAD + TIP), the executor re-enforcing the actor-bound AnyOf[Immutable, SenderInSlot]
affordances.mint_item: GATED (cap and state), None/root — pre-mint precondition (EPOCH == 0); the fire submits the FULL mint, the executor re-enforcing StrictMonotonic(EPOCH) 0->1
affordances.grant_custody: cap-only, None/root, carries the REAL Effect::GrantCapability (the manufacturer hands the custody cap forward NARROWED — the cap-graph half, derive_no_amplify)

deos.seam: CLOSED — the fire runs against the seeded item cell carrying the FULL custody program, so a stale-epoch handoff (StrictMonotonic), a non-signer baton flip (AnyOf[Immutable, SenderInSlot]), a WriteOnce link overwrite, and a HEAD rewind (Monotonic) all BITE in the deos fire / executor-submission path (tests/deos_seam.rs)
